description:
Model a residential building and design a rooftop solar array that strictly adheres to International Fire Code (IFC) 3-foot setback requirements. 

Building Specifications:
- Footprint: 32 feet (East-West/Red axis) × 24 feet (North-South/Green axis).
- Wall Height: 10 feet.
- Roof Type: Gable roof with ridge running East-West along the center.
- Roof Pitch: 6:12 (approx 26.6 degrees).
- Orientation: The 'front' is South-facing.

Design Constraints (The 'Setback'):
- On the South-facing roof slope, you must maintain a strict 3-foot clearance from all four edges (3 feet down from Ridge, 3 feet up from Eaves, 3 feet in from Left/East Rake, 3 feet in from Right/West Rake).
- Solar panels must be placed ONLY inside this central 3-foot setback 'safe zone'.

Instructions:
1. Model the building footprint and extrude walls to 10'.
2. Construct the gable roof with a 6:12 pitch.
3. On the South-facing roof face, use the Offset Tool or Tape Measure to create a boundary line exactly 3 feet inside all roof edges.
4. Select ONLY the inner 'safe zone' face.
5. Use the Skelion plugin to insert standard solar panels (Landscape orientation, Flush mount) into this zone. Ensure panels do not cross the 3-foot setback lines.
6. Save the model to `C:\Users\Docker\Documents\fire_code_solar.skp`.
7. Orient the camera to a Top View (Camera > Standard Views > Top) and ensure Parallel Projection is on (Camera > Parallel Projection) so the setbacks are clearly visible.
8. Export a 2D graphic of this view to `C:\Users\Docker\Documents\setback_verification.png`.
